description: In November 2022, the Cargo Truckers Solidarity union in South Korea went on an indefinite strike demanding the extension of a 'Safe Rates' system to combat rising fuel costs. The Yoon Suk-yeol administration took a hardline stance, declaring the strike illegal and eventually forcing them back to work, signaling a shift in government-union relations.

key_events.description: Government issues 'start-work' order for cement industry drivers.
Public complaints grow regarding supply chain disruptions.
Union votes to end the strike without securing the extension.
Government declares total victory over 'illegal' labor tactics.

calibration_notes: Highlights the impact of government intervention in labor disputes and its ability to flip middle-class sentiment against striking workers.
